title: manpage print space is messed up
body:
excerpt from <man:containers-storage(1)> below. prob. the line width is some how enforced while compile/build time? but this should be formatted, if the user actually want this, by the man reader on demand via `MANWITDH=` env variable. this makes reading the man page painful. thx, for your great work, though!

```txt
[...]
The  containers‐storage  command’s  features are broken down into
several subcommands:
 containers‐storage  add‐names(1)                    Add   layer,
image, or container name or names

containers‐storage applydiff(1)                   Apply a diff to
[...]
```

comments.body:
This is a widespread problem with all but one of the storage manual pages, I am working on fixing that.

Only containers-storage.conf.5.md is somewhat readable, but exhibits a problem similar to many other pages in the common and image docs. These files start with `%`, and use `#` for section headings like `SYNOPSIS` while it should be using `##` for that and replace the `%`  lines by a `#`.

common and image docs with broken headings, skipping lowercase titles to avoid false positives in code based on current main (01e98cc634dc):
<details>
<summary>$ git grep -ne '^# ' :{common,image}'/docs/*.md'</summary>

```
 git -P grep -nEe '^# [^a-z]+$' :{common,image}'/docs/*.md'
common/docs/Containerfile.5.md:3:# NAME
common/docs/Containerfile.5.md:7:# INTRODUCTION
common/docs/Containerfile.5.md:18:# SYNOPSIS
common/docs/Containerfile.5.md:26:# DESCRIPTION
common/docs/Containerfile.5.md:31:# USAGE
common/docs/Containerfile.5.md:55:# FORMAT
common/docs/Containerfile.5.md:687:# HISTORY
common/docs/containerignore.5.md:3:# NAME
common/docs/containerignore.5.md:7:# INTRODUCTION
common/docs/containerignore.5.md:23:# EXAMPLES
common/docs/containerignore.5.md:86:# HISTORY
common/docs/containers.conf.5.md:3:# NAME
common/docs/containers.conf.5.md:7:# DESCRIPTION
common/docs/containers.conf.5.md:72:# FORMAT
common/docs/containers.conf.5.md:1072:# FILES
common/docs/containers.conf.5.md:1097:# SEE ALSO
image/docs/containers-auth.json.5.md:3:# NAME
image/docs/containers-auth.json.5.md:6:# DESCRIPTION
image/docs/containers-auth.json.5.md:101:# SEE ALSO
image/docs/containers-auth.json.5.md:104:# HISTORY
image/docs/containers-certs.d.5.md:3:# NAME
image/docs/containers-certs.d.5.md:6:# DESCRIPTION
image/docs/containers-certs.d.5.md:32:# HISTORY
image/docs/containers-policy.json.5.md:5:# NAME
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md:5:# NAME
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md:8:# DESCRIPTION
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md:320:# NOTE: RISK OF USING UNQUALIFIED IMAGE NAMES
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md:341:# SEE ALSO
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md:344:# HISTORY
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.d.5.md:5:# NAME
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.d.5.md:8:# DESCRIPTION
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.d.5.md:14:# CONFIGURATION PRECEDENCE
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.d.5.md:32:# SEE ALSO
image/docs/containers-registries.conf.d.5.md:35:# HISTORY
image/docs/containers-registries.d.5.md:5:# NAME
image/docs/containers-registries.d.5.md:8:# DESCRIPTION
image/docs/containers-registries.d.5.md:138:# AUTHORS
image/docs/containers-signature.5.md:5:# NAME
image/docs/containers-signature.5.md:8:# DESCRIPTION
image/docs/containers-sigstore-signing-params.yaml.5.md:5:# NAME
image/docs/containers-sigstore-signing-params.yaml.5.md:8:# DESCRIPTION
image/docs/containers-sigstore-signing-params.yaml.5.md:88:# EXAMPLES
image/docs/containers-sigstore-signing-params.yaml.5.md:116:# SEE ALSO
```
</details>
For the record, I don’t see this behavior, neither before nor after #403. If it reproduces, what version of `go-md2man` is used?

I used go-md2man 2.0.7-1 on Arch Linux. Here is an example of one of the many pages that were broken before the PR:

```sh
$ git show HEAD~:storage/docs/containers-storage.md > old-containers-storage.md
$ go-md2man < old-containers-storage.md > old-containers-storage
$ go-md2man < containers-storage.md > containers-storage
$ diff -U2 --label OLD --label NEW old-containers-storage containers-storage
```
```diff
--- OLD
+++ NEW
@@ -1,5 +1,4 @@
 .nh
-
-.SH containers-storage 1 "August 2016"
+.TH containers-storage 1 "August 2016"
 .SH NAME
 containers-storage \- Manage layer/image/container storage
```

The old version looks like this:
```
containers‐storage − Manage layer/image/container storage

containers‐storage [subcommand] [‐‐help]

The   containers‐storage   command   is   a   front‐end  for  the
containers/storage library.  While  it  can  be  used  to  manage
storage  for  filesystem layers, images, and containers directly,
its main  use  cases  are  centered  around  troubleshooting  and
querying  the  state  of  storage which is being managed by other
processes.
```

The fixed version starts with this (`MANWIDTH=80 man ./containers-storage`):
```
containers-storage(1)       General Commands Manual       containers-storage(1)

NAME
       containers-storage - Manage layer/image/container storage

SYNOPSIS
       containers-storage [subcommand] [--help]

DESCRIPTION
       The containers-storage command is a front-end for the containers/storage
       library.   While it can be used to manage storage for filesystem layers,
       images, and containers directly, its main use cases are centered  around
       troubleshooting and querying the state of storage which is being managed
       by other processes.
```


The containers-storage.conf.5 manual page did not have such a severe readability issue, but still has an odd top bar:

```sh
$ git show HEAD~:storage/docs/containers-storage.conf.5.md > old-containers-storage.conf.5.md
$ go-md2man < old-containers-storage.conf.5.md > old-containers-storage.conf.5.md
$ go-md2man < old-containers-storage.conf.5.md > old-containers-storage.conf.5
$ diff -U2 --label OLD --label NEW old-containers-storage.conf.5 containers-storage.conf.5
```
```diff
--- OLD
+++ NEW
@@ -1,9 +1,6 @@
 .nh
-.TH containers-storage.conf(5) Container Storage Configuration File
-Dan Walsh
-May 2017
-
+.TH containers-storage.conf 5 "May 2017"
 .SH NAME
-storage.conf \- Syntax of Container Storage configuration file
+containers-storage.conf \- Syntax of Container Storage configuration file
 
 .SH DESCRIPTION
```

Old (`MANWIDTH=90 man ./old-containers-storage.conf.5`):
```
containers-storage.conf(5)(Container)      File     containers-storage.conf(5)(Container)

Dan Walsh May 2017

NAME
```

Fixed:
```
containers-storage.conf(5)         File Formats Manual         containers-storage.conf(5)

NAME
```
Thanks, reproduced now, on Linux. I have previously tried this on macOS, where the `man` formatter seems to be more forgiving.
